title: Peppery Norwegian Coconut Curry
description: A creamy, peppery Norwegian-inspired coconut curry with fermented coconut milk spheres, bright citrus, and a splash of soy sauce for a satvik-friendly monsoon twist. The dish balances heat and chill, giving it a shattering flavor profile.

ingredients.name: homemade red curry paste
ingredients.notes: ginger 1 tsp, cumin 1 tsp, coriander 1 tsp, dried chili 1 tsp

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large saucepan over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the ginger, lemongrass, and pepper crystals; sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the homemade curry paste and cook for another minute to release its flavors.
Pour in 400 ml coconut milk and 1 tbsp soy sauce, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Add diced bell pepper, carrot, and zucchini; simmer for 10 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Stir in lime juice and adjust seasoning with additional soy sauce if needed, then remove from heat.

While the curry rests, prepare coconut milk spheres by mixing 200 ml coconut milk with 1 tsp calcium lactate and 1 tsp sodium alginate, then use a syringe to drop the mixture into 1% calcium chloride bath for 90 seconds.
Gently lift the spheres with a slotted spoon, rinse briefly in cold water, and set aside.
Serve the curry in bowls, spoon a few coconut milk spheres on top, and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Enjoy the peppery, creamy, and refreshing Norwegian-inspired coconut curry.
